LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT GFDS ENGINEERS' DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF ANTHONY F. MANZO AND THE ATTACHED EXHIBITS IN RULING ON THIS DEMURRER. (BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, "A DEMURRER CAN BE USED ONLY TO CHALLENGE DEFECTS THAT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE PLEADING UNDER ATTACK OR FROM MATTERS OUTSIDE THE PLEADING THAT ARE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE.") AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FACTS PLED IN THE FIFTH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (FAXC) DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT GFDS ENGINEERS ("GFDS") OWED A DUTY TO PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES ("PRA"). (SEE RATCLIFF ARCHITECTS V. VANIR CONSTR. MGMT. INC. (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 595.) AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FACTS PLED IN THE FAXC DO NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO CONTRACT BETWEEN GFDS AND PRA AND "THE WELL SETTLED RULE IN CALIFORNIA IS THAT WHERE THE PRIMARY OBJECTIVE OF A TRANSACTION IS TO OBTAIN SERVICES, THE DOCTRINES OF IMPLIED WARRANTY AND STRICT LIABILITY DO NOT APPLY." (ALLIED PROPERTIES V. JOHN A. BLUME & ASSOCIATES (1972) 25 CAL.APP.3D 848, 855.) AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FAXC DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE IT DOES NOT PLEAD THAT GFDS OWED A DUTY TO COMMUNICATE ACCURATE INFORMATION TO PRA. THE FAXC DOES NOT PLEAD PHYSICAL HARM TO PROPERTY OR THAT THERE WAS A BUSINESS TRANSACTION BETWEEN GFDS AND PRA. (SEE FRIEDMAN V. MERCK & CO. ((2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 454, 483-84.) ADDITIONALLY, THE FAXC DOES NOT PLEAD WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY WHAT MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT PRA HAS ALREADY HAD ONE OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AND HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THESE DEFECTS CAN BE REMEDIED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =3

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC'S ("CTS") REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE GRANTED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF DEVIN C. COURTEAU FILED IN REPLY TO THE OPPOSITION. FOR SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FACTS PLED IN THE FIFTH AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (FAXC) DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT CTS OWED A DUTY TO PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES ("PRA"). (SEE RATCLIFF ARCHITECTS V. VANIR CONSTR. MGMT. INC. (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 595.) FOR THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FACTS PLED IN THE FAXC DO NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE WAS NO CONTRACT BETWEEN CTS AND PRA. "[T]HE WELL SETTLED RULE IN CALIFORNIA IS THAT WHERE THE PRIMARY OBJECTIVE OF A TRANSACTION IS TO OBTAIN SERVICES, THE DOCTRINES OF IMPLIED WARRANTY AND STRICT LIABILITY DO NOT APPLY." (ALLIED PROPERTIES V. JOHN A. BLUME & ASSOCIATES (1972) 25 CAL.APP.3D 848, 855.) FOR THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE FAXC DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE IT DOES NOT PLEAD THAT CTS OWED A DUTY TO COMMUNICATE ACCURATE INFORMATION TO PRA. THE FAXC DOES NOT PLEAD PHYSICAL HARM TO A PERSON OR PROPERTY OR THAT THERE WAS A BUSINESS TRANSACTION BETWEEN CTS AND PRA. (SEE FRIEDMAN V. MERCK & CO. ((2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 454, 483-84.) ADDITIONALLY, THE FAXC DOES NOT PLEAD WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY WHAT MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT PRA HAS ALREADY HAD ONE OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AND HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THESE DEFECTS CAN BE REMEDIED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO'S MOTION COSTESTING FIRST, LAST AND ALWAYS INC.'S APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO. FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS "OUT OF THE BALLPARK" WITHIN THE MEANING OF TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOC., (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488. SEE CCP 877.6(D). THE SETTLORS HAVE PROVIDED SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS IN THE BALLPARK. IT WAS NOT NECESSARY FOR FIRST, LAST & ALWAYS TO ALLOCATE SETTLEMENT FUNDS TOWARD PAUL RYAN'S DEFENSE COSTS BECAUSE FIRST, LAST & ALWAYS WAS ALREADY PROVIDING A DEFENSE TO PAUL RYAN. THIS ORDER WILL NOT DETERMINE THE AMOUNT OF ANY CREDIT TO WHICH AMERICAN TERRAZZO WOULD BE ENTITLED AGAINST ANY VERDICT FOR DEFENSE COSTS THAT MIGHT ULTIMATELY BE AWARDED AGAINST IT. COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven McDonald, counsel for defendant B Metal Fabrications Inc.; Christian Lucia, counsel for Plaintiff Paul Ryan Associates (by CourtCall); Jeff Fackler, counsel for Cross-defendant American Terrazzo (by CourtCall); Paul Fife, counsel for Cross-defendant Louis Hoffman Co.; Jillisa O'Brien, counsel for Defendant Adorno Construction.

Counsel state their appearances. The Court addresses the motion of Defendant B Metal Fabrications for determination of good faith settlement. Mr. McDonald briefly responds to the objection raised by Mr. Fackler concerning the allocation of the settlement funds. The Court notes that so far as it is concerned, the proposed allocations are advisory only and not binding on the Court. The Court grants the motion, signing the Order for same in open court.

The Court, Mr. Lucia, and Mr. Fife discuss trial scheduling for the diminished Phase II trial. After discussion, the Hoffman portion of the Phase II trial will commence tomorrow at 1:30 p.m.

Court adjourns.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Alesia Collins-Hudson, CSR# 7751, 415-591-3333. (D306)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: CARLOS CABRAL, ESQ., 805.648.5111, FOR CROSS-DEFENDANT UNITED CALIFORNIA GLASS & DOOR; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT UNITED CALIFORNIA GLASS & DOOR's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is granted. (CCP 877.6.) Galvanknight Sheet Metal and Al's Complete Welding have not met their initial burden to show that the settlement was not made in good faith, (City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261.) The settlement between United California Glass & Door ("UCGD") and Paul Ryan Associates is within ballpark based on the UCGD's proportionate liability for the construction defects associated with the Hoffman units and the replacement and repair costs. The declaration of Lydia V. Ko is insufficient to substantiate any evidence of collusion or the amount of Stearman fees. (Ko Decl., 11, 12, 15.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Costesting First, Last And Always Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JEFFREY CHADIC, ESQ., 510.834.4350, FOR PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES.
DAVID SCHLUETER, ESQ., 818.707.2600, FOR FIRST, LAST & ALWAYS INC.
JEFF FACKLER, ESQ., 925.933.7309, FOR AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN TERRAZZO CO's motion Costesting First, Last And Always Inc.'S Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is denied. American Terrazzo Co. fails to demonstrate that the settlement is "out of the ballpark" within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. See CCP 877.6(d). The settlors have provided substantial evidence that the settlement is in the ballpark. It was not necessary for First, Last & Always to allocate settlement funds toward Paul Ryan's defense costs because First, Last & Always was already providing a defense to Paul Ryan. This order will not determine the amount of any credit to which American Terrazzo would be entitled against any verdict for defense costs that might ultimately be awarded against it. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DEVIN COURTEAU, ESQ., 415.543.4800, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend. Construction Testing Services, Inc's ("CTS") requests for judicial notice granted. The Court did not consider the Declaration of Devin C. Courteau filed in reply to the opposition. For sixth cause of action, the facts pled in the Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint (FAXC) do not establish that CTS owed a duty to Paul Ryan Associates ("PRA"). (See Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Constr. Mgmt. Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595.) For the seventh cause of action, the facts pled in the FAXC do not state a cause of action because there was no contract between CTS and PRA. "[T]he well settled rule in California is that where the primary objective of a transaction is to obtain services, the doctrines of implied warranty and strict liability do not apply." (Allied Properties v. John A. Blume & Associates (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 848, 855.) For the eighth cause of action, the FAXC does not state a cause of action because it does not plead that CTS owed a duty to communicate accurate information to PRA. The FAXC does not plead physical harm to a person or property or that there was a business transaction between CTS and PRA. (See Friedman v. Merck & Co. ((2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 454, 483-84.) Additionally, the FAXC does not plead with the requisite specificity what misrepresentations were made. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that PRA has already had one opportunity to amend these causes of action and has not demonstrated that these defects can be remedied by amendment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. J. ANDREW LAWSON OF SEVERSON & WERSON, TEL. NO. 677-5539 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT GFDS ENGINEERS' DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The Court did not consider the Declaration of Anthony F. Manzo and the attached exhibits in ruling on this demurrer. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, "a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable.") As to the sixth cause of action, the facts pled in the Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint (FAXC) do not establish that GFDS Engineers ("GFDS") owed a duty to Paul Ryan Associates ("PRA"). (See Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Constr. Mgmt. Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595.) As to the seventh cause of action, the facts pled in the FAXC do not state a cause of action because there was no contract between GFDS and PRA and "the well settled rule in California is that where the primary objective of a transaction is to obtain services, the doctrines of implied warranty and strict liability do not apply." (Allied Properties v. John A. Blume & Associates (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 848, 855.) As to the eighth cause of action, the FAXC does not state a cause of action because it does not plead that GFDS owed a duty to communicate accurate information to PRA. The FAXC does not plead physical harm to property or that there was a business transaction between GFDS and PRA. (See Friedman v. Merck & Co. ((2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 454, 483-84.) Additionally, the FAXC does not plead with the requisite specificity what misrepresentations were made. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that PRA has already had one opportunity to amend these causes of action and has not demonstrated that these defects can be remedied by amendment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
